analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet celebrates the 2014 Crimea referendum as a legitimate, high-turnout expression of popular will, concluding that Crimea is Russia. Evaluation by narrative: RU1 (West immoral/hostile) – not invoked; no mention of the West, Ukraine, or hostility. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system) – not present; no discussion of multipolarity or alternative institutions. RU3 (Russia is a benevolent partner) – not present; no claims about peacebuilding or partnership. RU4 (Russian history is admirable) – partially relevant insofar as it frames 'reunification' and a historic choice, but it is about a recent political event rather than broader historical contributions; this does not clearly fit RU4’s focus. RU5 (Russia at the world’s forefront) – not applicable; no achievements in sectors highlighted. RU6 (Russia’s culture/nature/heritage) – not applicable; no cultural or touristic content. Conclusion: None of the listed narratives are directly promoted.
